commit.commitMessage: perf(mobile): make search instant across all content lists

subactions.command:
#!/usr/bin/env bash

cd apps/mobile && flutter packages pub get

#!/usr/bin/env bash

# --build-number is explicit and derived from a Unix timestamp
# rather than relying on flutter.versionCode (from pubspec.yaml's
# version: x.y.z+N) - two builds produced identical versionCode
# 2001 back to back, and Android silently refuses to install an
# "update" whose versionCode isn't strictly higher than what's
# already installed, so a real user re-downloading a fixed APK
# got no error and no update, just the same broken app still
# running. $CM_BUILD_NUMBER was tried first but is empty when a
# build is triggered via the direct REST API rather than a
# webhook/dashboard click ("buildNumber:  was not a valid integer
# value" build failure) - date +%s is always populated.
cd apps/mobile && flutter build appbundle --release \
  --build-number=$(date +%s) \
  --dart-define=ANILIST_CLIENT_ID=${ANILIST_CLIENT_ID:-} \
  --dart-define=GOOGLE_WEB_CLIENT_ID=${GOOGLE_WEB_CLIENT_ID:-}

#!/usr/bin/env bash

# Plain --target-platform android-arm64 only restricts Flutter's
# own engine binary. --split-per-abi activates Android's actual
# splits.abi packaging mechanism, which filters every native lib
# regardless of source. Combined with --target-platform, only one
# output file is produced: app-arm64-v8a-release.apk.
# If a separate Android-TV build is ever wanted, add a second
# android-tv-release workflow passing:
#   flutter build apk --release --target-platform android-arm64
#   --split-per-abi --dart-define=IS_TV_BUILD=true
cd apps/mobile && flutter build apk --release --target-platform android-arm64 --split-per-abi \
  --build-number=$(date +%s) \
  --dart-define=ANILIST_CLIENT_ID=${ANILIST_CLIENT_ID:-} \
  --dart-define=GOOGLE_WEB_CLIENT_ID=${GOOGLE_WEB_CLIENT_ID:-}

buildActions.name: Publish release APK to R2 (updates the website download link)

subactions.command:
#!/usr/bin/env bash

cd apps/mobile
pip3 install --quiet --break-system-packages boto3 2>/dev/null || pip3 install --quiet boto3
# Root-caused a real incident: this step used to read the just-built
# APK immediately after the prior script exited, but on Codemagic's
# build filesystem the file wasn't always fully flushed/visible yet
# by the time this step's `open()` ran - it silently read a stale,
# incomplete, or previous-run copy instead of erroring, and uploaded
# THAT to production. Confirmed by comparing this step's uploaded
# bytes against Codemagic's own build-artifact copy of the exact
# same file (from the SAME build) - they differed, proving the
# Flutter/Gradle build itself was always correct and this read was
# the actual bug. `sync` + a poll-until-content-hash-is-stable loop
# + a zip-integrity check before upload eliminates the race instead
# of masking it with a blind sleep.
sync
python3 - <<'PYEOF'
import hashlib
import os
import time
import zipfile
import boto3
from botocore.client import Config

# Newer botocore (as freshly pip-installed on the CI machine, vs.
# an older version) defaults to computing/sending extra request
# checksum headers for S3 calls, which R2's signature validation
# doesn't handle correctly - causing SignatureDoesNotMatch on
# both multipart AND plain PutObject. Confirmed by reproducing
# locally with the same botocore version and verifying these two
# Config flags (restoring pre-default-checksum behavior) fix it
# against the real bucket before pushing this.
# Strip defensively - a stray trailing newline/space picked up
# when pasting a secret into Codemagic's env var UI is enough to
# break S3 signature calculation (SignatureDoesNotMatch) while
# leaving the access key id "valid-looking", which is exactly
# what happened on a prior run of this exact script/code.
s3_endpoint = os.environ["S3_ENDPOINT"].strip()
s3_access_key_id = os.environ["S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID"].strip()
s3_secret_access_key = os.environ["S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"].strip()
s3_bucket = os.environ["S3_BUCKET"].strip()
print(f"[diag] endpoint={s3_endpoint!r} access_key_id len={len(s3_access_key_id)} secret len={len(s3_secret_access_key)} bucket={s3_bucket!r}")

s3 = boto3.client(
    "s3",
    endpoint_url=s3_endpoint,
    aws_access_key_id=s3_access_key_id,
    aws_secret_access_key=s3_secret_access_key,
    region_name="auto",
    config=Config(
        signature_version="s3v4",
        request_checksum_calculation="when_required",
        response_checksum_validation="when_required",
    ),
)
apk_path = "build/app/outputs/flutter-apk/app-arm64-v8a-release.apk"
key = "downloads/naijaspride-android-latest.apk"

def sha256_of(path):
    h = hashlib.sha256()
    with open(path, "rb") as fh:
        for chunk in iter(lambda: fh.read(1 << 20), b""):
            h.update(chunk)
    return h.hexdigest()

# Poll until the file's size + hash are stable across two reads a
# second apart - a still-being-written file will change between
# samples; a fully-flushed one won't. Caps at 10 attempts (~10s).
prev_sig = None
for attempt in range(10):
    size = os.path.getsize(apk_path)
    digest = sha256_of(apk_path)
    sig = (size, digest)
    if sig == prev_sig:
        break
    prev_sig = sig
    time.sleep(1)
else:
    raise SystemExit(f"APK content never stabilized after {attempt + 1} attempts - refusing to publish a possibly-still-writing file")

size, digest = prev_sig
print(f"[diag] {apk_path}: {size} bytes, sha256={digest}")

# A truncated/mid-write zip fails this even if size/hash looked
# momentarily stable between two fast reads - the definitive check.
with zipfile.ZipFile(apk_path) as zf:
    bad = zf.testzip()
    if bad is not None:
        raise SystemExit(f"APK failed zip integrity check at {bad} - refusing to publish a corrupt file")
    if "lib/arm64-v8a/libapp.so" not in zf.namelist():
        raise SystemExit("APK is missing lib/arm64-v8a/libapp.so - refusing to publish")

# Cloudflare (fronting the R2 custom domain) was found caching this
# exact object aggressively and INCONSISTENTLY across edge nodes -
# after a confirmed-correct upload (verified via origin ETag), the
# public URL kept serving stale, and even truncated, bytes for
# minutes afterward, with zero way to force-purge (no Cloudflare
# API token available). no-cache/must-revalidate tells Cloudflare
# to always revalidate with origin before serving instead of
# trusting a cached copy - the correct fix for a URL that's
# expected to change contents under the same filename on every
# release, instead of routing around it with a new filename each
# time (which was used as an emergency one-off fix for the
# already-poisoned "latest.apk" cache entry, not a real solution).
with open(apk_path, "rb") as f:
    s3.put_object(
        Bucket=s3_bucket,
        Key=key,
        Body=f,
        ContentType="application/vnd.android.package-archive",
        CacheControl="no-cache, must-revalidate",
    )
print(f"Published {apk_path} (sha256={digest}) to r2://{s3_bucket}/{key}")
PYEOF

        signature_version="s3v4",
        request_checksum_calculation="when_required",
        response_checksum_validation="when_required",
    ),
)
apk_path = "build/app/outputs/flutter-apk/app-arm64-v8a-release.apk"
key = "downloads/naijaspride-android-latest.apk"

def sha256_of(path):
    h = hashlib.sha256()
    with open(path, "rb") as fh:
        for chunk in iter(lambda: fh.read(1 << 20), b""):
            h.update(chunk)
    return h.hexdigest()

# Poll until the file's size + hash are stable across two reads a
# second apart - a still-being-written file will change between
# samples; a fully-flushed one won't. Caps at 10 attempts (~10s).
prev_sig = None
for attempt in range(10):
    size = os.path.getsize(apk_path)
    digest = sha256_of(apk_path)
    sig = (size, digest)
    if sig == prev_sig:
        break
    prev_sig = sig
    time.sleep(1)
else:
    raise SystemExit(f"APK content never stabilized after {attempt + 1} attempts - refusing to publish a possibly-still-writing file")

size, digest = prev_sig
print(f"[diag] {apk_path}: {size} bytes, sha256={digest}")

# A truncated/mid-write zip fails this even if size/hash looked
# momentarily stable between two fast reads - the definitive check.
with zipfile.ZipFile(apk_path) as zf:
    bad = zf.testzip()
    if bad is not None:
        raise SystemExit(f"APK failed zip integrity check at {bad} - refusing to publish a corrupt file")
    if "lib/arm64-v8a/libapp.so" not in zf.namelist():
        raise SystemExit("APK is missing lib/arm64-v8a/libapp.so - refusing to publish")

# Cloudflare (fronting the R2 custom domain) was found caching this
# exact object aggressively and INCONSISTENTLY across edge nodes -
# after a confirmed-correct upload (verified via origin ETag), the
# public URL kept serving stale, and even truncated, bytes for
# minutes afterward, with zero way to force-purge (no Cloudflare
# API token available). no-cache/must-revalidate tells Cloudflare
# to always revalidate with origin before serving instead of
# trusting a cached copy - the correct fix for a URL that's
# expected to change contents under the same filename on every
# release, instead of routing around it with a new filename each
# time (which was used as an emergency one-off fix for the
# already-poisoned "latest.apk" cache entry, not a real solution).
with open(apk_path, "rb") as f:
    s3.put_object(
        Bucket=s3_bucket,
        Key=key,
        Body=f,
        ContentType="application/vnd.android.package-archive",
        CacheControl="no-cache, must-revalidate",
    )
print(f"Published {apk_path} (sha256={digest}) to r2://{s3_bucket}/{key}")
PYEOF

commit.commitMessage: fix(mobile): fix missing back buttons, unreadable text, and broken embed streams

subactions.command:
#!/usr/bin/env bash

cd apps/mobile && flutter packages pub get

#!/usr/bin/env bash

# --build-number is explicit and derived from a Unix timestamp
# rather than relying on flutter.versionCode (from pubspec.yaml's
# version: x.y.z+N) - two builds produced identical versionCode
# 2001 back to back, and Android silently refuses to install an
# "update" whose versionCode isn't strictly higher than what's
# already installed, so a real user re-downloading a fixed APK
# got no error and no update, just the same broken app still
# running. $CM_BUILD_NUMBER was tried first but is empty when a
# build is triggered via the direct REST API rather than a
# webhook/dashboard click ("buildNumber:  was not a valid integer
# value" build failure) - date +%s is always populated.
cd apps/mobile && flutter build appbundle --release \
  --build-number=$(date +%s) \
  --dart-define=ANILIST_CLIENT_ID=${ANILIST_CLIENT_ID:-} \
  --dart-define=GOOGLE_WEB_CLIENT_ID=${GOOGLE_WEB_CLIENT_ID:-}

#!/usr/bin/env bash

# Plain --target-platform android-arm64 only restricts Flutter's
# own engine binary. --split-per-abi activates Android's actual
# splits.abi packaging mechanism, which filters every native lib
# regardless of source. Combined with --target-platform, only one
# output file is produced: app-arm64-v8a-release.apk.
# If a separate Android-TV build is ever wanted, add a second
# android-tv-release workflow passing:
#   flutter build apk --release --target-platform android-arm64
#   --split-per-abi --dart-define=IS_TV_BUILD=true
cd apps/mobile && flutter build apk --release --target-platform android-arm64 --split-per-abi \
  --build-number=$(date +%s) \
  --dart-define=ANILIST_CLIENT_ID=${ANILIST_CLIENT_ID:-} \
  --dart-define=GOOGLE_WEB_CLIENT_ID=${GOOGLE_WEB_CLIENT_ID:-}

buildActions.name: Publish release APK to R2 (updates the website download link)

subactions.command:
#!/usr/bin/env bash

cd apps/mobile
pip3 install --quiet --break-system-packages boto3 2>/dev/null || pip3 install --quiet boto3
# Root-caused a real incident: this step used to read the just-built
# APK immediately after the prior script exited, but on Codemagic's
# build filesystem the file wasn't always fully flushed/visible yet
# by the time this step's `open()` ran - it silently read a stale,
# incomplete, or previous-run copy instead of erroring, and uploaded
# THAT to production. Confirmed by comparing this step's uploaded
# bytes against Codemagic's own build-artifact copy of the exact
# same file (from the SAME build) - they differed, proving the
# Flutter/Gradle build itself was always correct and this read was
# the actual bug. `sync` + a poll-until-content-hash-is-stable loop
# + a zip-integrity check before upload eliminates the race instead
# of masking it with a blind sleep.
sync
python3 - <<'PYEOF'
import hashlib
import os
import time
import zipfile
import boto3
from botocore.client import Config

# Newer botocore (as freshly pip-installed on the CI machine, vs.
# an older version) defaults to computing/sending extra request
# checksum headers for S3 calls, which R2's signature validation
# doesn't handle correctly - causing SignatureDoesNotMatch on
# both multipart AND plain PutObject. Confirmed by reproducing
# locally with the same botocore version and verifying these two
# Config flags (restoring pre-default-checksum behavior) fix it
# against the real bucket before pushing this.
# Strip defensively - a stray trailing newline/space picked up
# when pasting a secret into Codemagic's env var UI is enough to
# break S3 signature calculation (SignatureDoesNotMatch) while
# leaving the access key id "valid-looking", which is exactly
# what happened on a prior run of this exact script/code.
s3_endpoint = os.environ["S3_ENDPOINT"].strip()
s3_access_key_id = os.environ["S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID"].strip()
s3_secret_access_key = os.environ["S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"].strip()
s3_bucket = os.environ["S3_BUCKET"].strip()
print(f"[diag] endpoint={s3_endpoint!r} access_key_id len={len(s3_access_key_id)} secret len={len(s3_secret_access_key)} bucket={s3_bucket!r}")

s3 = boto3.client(
    "s3",
    endpoint_url=s3_endpoint,
    aws_access_key_id=s3_access_key_id,
    aws_secret_access_key=s3_secret_access_key,
    region_name="auto",
    config=Config(
        signature_version="s3v4",
        request_checksum_calculation="when_required",
        response_checksum_validation="when_required",
    ),
)
apk_path = "build/app/outputs/flutter-apk/app-arm64-v8a-release.apk"
key = "downloads/naijaspride-android-latest.apk"

def sha256_of(path):
    h = hashlib.sha256()
    with open(path, "rb") as fh:
        for chunk in iter(lambda: fh.read(1 << 20), b""):
            h.update(chunk)
    return h.hexdigest()

# Poll until the file's size + hash are stable across two reads a
# second apart - a still-being-written file will change between
# samples; a fully-flushed one won't. Caps at 10 attempts (~10s).
prev_sig = None
for attempt in range(10):
    size = os.path.getsize(apk_path)
    digest = sha256_of(apk_path)
    sig = (size, digest)
    if sig == prev_sig:
        break
    prev_sig = sig
    time.sleep(1)
else:
    raise SystemExit(f"APK content never stabilized after {attempt + 1} attempts - refusing to publish a possibly-still-writing file")

size, digest = prev_sig
print(f"[diag] {apk_path}: {size} bytes, sha256={digest}")

# A truncated/mid-write zip fails this even if size/hash looked
# momentarily stable between two fast reads - the definitive check.
with zipfile.ZipFile(apk_path) as zf:
    bad = zf.testzip()
    if bad is not None:
        raise SystemExit(f"APK failed zip integrity check at {bad} - refusing to publish a corrupt file")
    if "lib/arm64-v8a/libapp.so" not in zf.namelist():
        raise SystemExit("APK is missing lib/arm64-v8a/libapp.so - refusing to publish")

# Cloudflare (fronting the R2 custom domain) was found caching this
# exact object aggressively and INCONSISTENTLY across edge nodes -
# after a confirmed-correct upload (verified via origin ETag), the
# public URL kept serving stale, and even truncated, bytes for
# minutes afterward, with zero way to force-purge (no Cloudflare
# API token available). no-cache/must-revalidate tells Cloudflare
# to always revalidate with origin before serving instead of
# trusting a cached copy - the correct fix for a URL that's
# expected to change contents under the same filename on every
# release, instead of routing around it with a new filename each
# time (which was used as an emergency one-off fix for the
# already-poisoned "latest.apk" cache entry, not a real solution).
with open(apk_path, "rb") as f:
    s3.put_object(
        Bucket=s3_bucket,
        Key=key,
        Body=f,
        ContentType="application/vnd.android.package-archive",
        CacheControl="no-cache, must-revalidate",
    )
print(f"Published {apk_path} (sha256={digest}) to r2://{s3_bucket}/{key}")
PYEOF
